compiled_state.title: Test isolation + brittleness hardening (tmp markers, monkeypatch restore, jitter invariants)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What (SDET review I5/I6/I7)
- I6: tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py:73-80 deletes FIXED global paths incl. /tmp/.ticket-sync-fallback (not namespaced) — two such tests / xdist workers / aborted-run leftovers can perturb each other. Namespace under tmp_path; never touch a global /tmp fallback.
- I7: test_advisory_lock_cas_race.py:158 (and the non-CAS path) assign lock_mod._git_run=... with NO restore, relying on fresh-module-load-per-test — leaks if fixture scope changes. Use monkeypatch.setattr everywhere (auto-restored).
- I5: test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py:131-161 over-pins magic constants (expected_bases=[0.2,0.4,0.8,1.6,3.2]); any backoff tuning breaks it though behavior is correct. Assert structural invariants (5 sleeps, each >0, <= cap, monotonic non-decreasing) instead.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /tmp sync markers namespaced under tmp_path (no global-path deletion)
- [ ] All monkeypatching uses auto-restoring monkeypatch.setattr (no raw attribute assignment without restore)
- [ ] Jitter test asserts structural invariants, not pinned constants
- [ ] Order-independence verified (passes under pytest-randomly)
